calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-19-2003 Vacated

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-19-2003 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for hearing this day for Settlement Hearing.
PTJ: Junji Suzuki, Clerks: P. Dias/M. Vallejo, CSR: Carlito Mandia.
James Blanding, Plaintiff present present with counsel, John C. Brown.
Luis Lory, Jose Sanchez and John Lory, Defendants present in pro per.
Parties met the chambers and reached a settlement by stipulation.
Terms of the ORAL settlement were read into the record as follows:
Plaintiff is entitled to a (future) judgment against the defendant for restitution and (immediate) possession of the premises of the property located at:
1225 Palou Avenue, San Francisco, CA.
Forfeiture of the rental agreement.
Securtiy deposit is forfeited.
Defendants to deliver the keys and garage door to plaintiff's agent at 1674 Revere Ave.
If the defendant vacates the premises on or before July 7, 2003.
Security deposit of $1600.00 shall be returned according to law without interest.
Each party waives claim against the other.
Each to bears its own costs and attorney's fees.
Any items left in the premises will be disposed of.
No one else occupying the premises who claims a right to possession.
If defendant did not comply with the agreement, plaintiff may so state by ex parte declaration, with (24) hours notice to the defendant and have judgment entered.
Defendant waives any right to make a motion to set aside or vacate the judgment, or to apply for a stay or relief from forfeiture.
All parties agreed to the settlement in open court.
The Court deemed the matter settled and trial date is vacated.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-08-2003 Advanced From May-28-03 To May-20-03 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 301

ORDER ADVANCING TIME TO HEAR DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-20-2003 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
John C. Brown, Esq. of REDENBACHER & BROWN 409-8600, for plaintiff and opposing party

RULING:
No other appearance on this matter. Tentative ruling adopted -- DEFENDANT NISAA
HASSAN'S DEMURRER COMPLAINT -- Overruled; 5 days leave to answer. ORDER SIGNED IN
